lowest20.message: Drop-off blew up again and I'm spiraling about tomorrow.
lowest20.chapter: Right now, this family is in a chapter of trying to make daily life feel steadier for Max, especially around Mornings are harder when sleep is poor and transitions feel rushed. and Crowded noise often raises anxiety and lowers demand tolerance.. There are signs of real movement: small wins are showing up more often. What is still hard is School drop-off distress and Morning transition to school felt rushed, and that can make progress feel fragile even when it is real. Emotionally, the parent is carrying a heavy mix of worry, fatigue, and pressure to get it right, while still continuing to show up with care. What has helped is transition preview and offering two choices. What should not be forgotten is Max's strengths in kind and curious.
lowest20.response:
That sounds worrying. You're not alone in this.

What may be happening right now is that everyone is running on low capacity, so even small demands can feel much bigger. What matters now is one steadier moment, not a perfect fix.

It makes sense that this is weighing on you.

Tomorrow can be simpler. School felt a bit more manageable for Max today than recently. Parenting a neurodivergent child asks a lot of you. Feeling stretched doesn't mean you're doing it wrong.

lowest20.message: We had a setback after better days and I feel like we're back at the start. How do we recover without panic?
lowest20.chapter: Right now, this family is in a chapter of trying to make daily life feel steadier for Leo, especially around Mornings are harder when sleep is poor and transitions feel rushed. and Crowded noise often raises anxiety and lowers demand tolerance.. There are signs of real movement: On 3 April, asked for help before escalation, On 3 April, recovered faster after school, and Asked for help before escalation. What is still hard is Meltdown recovery took longer than hoped and Activity switch conflict, and that can make progress feel fragile even when it is real. Emotionally, the parent is carrying The meltdown was intense and I feel discouraged again., while still continuing to show up with care. What has helped is transition preview and offering two choices. What should not be forgotten is Leo's strengths in kind and curious.
